---
title: "Why does AI Slop Make You So Angry?"
description: "A Cogntitive and Neurological Perspective"
bibliography: references.bib
author:
  - name: Z Skigen, Jazelle Saligumba
    affiliation: Neuroscience @ Pomona College
    affiliation-url: https://www.pomona.edu/academics/departments/neuroscience
date: 05-05-2026
categories: [AI Images, AI Slop, Neuroscience]
citation:
  url: https://zskigen.github.io/posts/2026-05-05-ai-slop/
image: man.png
draft: false
---

Ah. Another morning where, against my better judgement, I check my phone, open Instagram, and encounter a meme, or some sort of image. Whatever it is, it is almost certain I encounter something artificially generated; usually usually *piss filtered*[^1], cartoonish, yet imprecise imagery.

[^1]: (artificial intelligence, slang) A usually undesired yellow tint commonly appearing in images produced by generative AI. [@wiktionary_piss_filter]

As I created this project, I wanted to ground my research in an almost indescribable feeling that never fails to beholden me every time I encounter an AI image. Words to describe such a feeling include: rage, discomfort, annoyance, and the main focus of this essay, a feeling of uncanny valley. This follows mori (1970) in response to robots, i believe AI has a similar effect. I wondered if anyone else shared a similar experience, and Reddit threads confirm

### Case Studies

![man](man.png){width=300}

![storefront](storefront.png){width=300}

![bar](bar.png){width=300}

![picaxe](picaxe.png){width=300}

### Fluency Theory and Predictive Processing

neuroscience stuff with all my citations

### Benjamin and Aura

make it philosophical

### Why do people like AI art at all?

propose the question of why people even like ai at all, people enjoy the art sometimes, facebook, ai comment bots, 

### Conclusion

idk
